items.title: US Stocks To Open Higher On Shortened Trading Day: Santa Claus Rally Period Officially Begins, Says Analyst
items.author: Rishabh Mishra
items.description: US stock futures are trading higher ahead of a shortened trading session on Christmas Eve. Analysts expect a 'Santa Rally' during this period, which has historically seen positive returns. However, they also caution about potential market corrections in 2025 due to stretched valuations.

items.title: Dow Jones: A Relevant Benchmark or a Relic of the Past?
items.author: Marketbeat.Com
items.description: The article discusses the relevance of the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) as a benchmark for the US stock market, compared to the S&P 500 index. It argues that the DJIA, with only 30 stocks, may no longer accurately represent the broader market, and the S&P 500 should be considered a more comprehensive and accurate barometer.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article argues that the S&P 500 index, with 500 stocks, is a more accurate representation of the US stock market and should be the primary benchmark used by investors and fund managers.
The article mentions the Nasdaq 100 index as another major index, but does not make a strong argument for or against its relevance as a benchmark.

The article highlights the Invesco S&P 500 Equal-Weight ETF as a way to get an even playing field, as it tracks the S&P 500 index with equal weighting for each constituent, rather than market capitalization weighting.

The article suggests that the DJIA, with only 30 stocks, may not be an accurate representation of the broader US stock market, and that the S&P 500 index should be considered a more comprehensive and accurate benchmark.
